response:
The total time function combines all three database response models line.
Each term uses the specific query count assigned to that database line.
MySQL time depends on the square and exponential of its share line.
Oracle time relies on the logarithm and inverse of its share line.
Sybase time involves the one point five power minus the root line.
We sum these three individual expressions to get the total time line.
Optimization requires finding the values of alpha and beta line.
We take partial derivatives with respect to both distribution variables line.
Setting these derivatives to zero yields the necessary equations line.
Solving these equations numerically provides the optimal proportions line.
The constants given define the specific shape of each curve line.
With one thousand queries the system must balance the load line.
Calculations show that alpha should be roughly zero point two five line.
The original value for beta is approximately zero point three five line.
These proportions minimize the overall latency for the system line.
